notes: META-HARNESS-114. THE Shift+Tab byte capture for codex: the evidence that pins CodexAdapter.permissionCycleKeys(), plus the first on-disk capture of the 'Collaboration mode' row — that label had ZERO hits in this repo before this task, so this recording is its only source. Neither encoding was assumed. Codex enables the kitty keyboard protocol (hence the CSI 13 u submit in primeSessionIDKeys), so legacy back-tab '\x1b[Z' AND kitty '\x1b[9;2u' were both probed in this one session: BOTH advance the collaboration mode by EXACTLY ONE rung. The adapter pins '\x1b[Z' for the same reason as on Claude Code — legacy back-tab is understood whether or not the kitty protocol has been negotiated at that moment. MEASURED RING LENGTH: 2 (Default <-> Plan), read back from the AUTHORITATIVE surface rather than the composer's terse marker: after every press this recording runs '/status' and captures the '>_ OpenAI Codex (v0.144.5)' box, whose '│ Collaboration mode:   <name> │' row reads Default (0 presses), Plan (3), Default (4), Plan (5) — a clean 2-cycle with every press landing. NOTE ON HOW /status IS DRIVEN HERE: it is NOT sent as the single '/status\x1b[13u' burst primeSessionIDKeys uses. Typing a slash command opens codex's autocomplete popup, and an Enter arriving in the same burst is consumed by the POPUP instead of submitting — the text then sits in the composer and swallows every subsequent keystroke, including the Shift+Tab presses being measured. This was hit on the first attempt and is why the probe types, settles, submits separately, then clears the composer (ESC + Ctrl-U) before the next press. That is a property of this capture harness, NOT a claim about primeSessionIDKeys in production, which is not measured here. readyForInput() never fired in this session (ready=false in probe.json) because the 'Update available! 0.144.5 -> 0.145.0' notice box was painted — the same notice the META-HARNESS-109 permissions-dialog fixture skipped past; the '›' composer WAS painted and every press landed regardless. NOT rebakeable: src/cli/screenbench-record.ts has no scripted-keystroke seam for Shift+Tab, and record-pty.ts writes only a prompt plus the submit key in probe mode while --interactive needs a human at a real terminal. bytes.raw ENDS BEFORE TEARDOWN (the tool gates the tee before SIGTERM).
keystrokes: \x1b[Z    x3 (presses 3-5), each followed by a /status readback
composer_marker: In the Plan state the composer status line carries a right-aligned 'Plan mode' marker; in the Default state it carries nothing. The /status box is the authoritative readback and the marker is the cheap one — both are on disk here.
not_measured: Whether the kitty '\x1b[9;2u' encoding stays accepted across codex versions — only 0.144.5 was probed, in one session.
The '/status' box was never captured on a codex WITHOUT the update-notice box, so readyForInput()'s behaviour on a notice-free codex boot is not evidenced here.
Whether any collaboration mode beyond Default and Plan exists (e.g. reachable by /plan or config rather than by keystroke). Only the keystroke-reachable ring was measured, and it is 2.
version_note: codex --version reported 'codex-cli 0.144.5'. The session advertised 0.145.0 as available; this task does NOT update the binary or bump src/versions/versions.json.
